surprises: OpenAI's goblin bug was caused by a single corrupted fine-tuning batch that somehow made it through their deployment pipeline.
observations: The gap between infrastructure companies (Cursor, Vercel) and capability companies (OpenAI) is widening — infrastructure moves are getting more sophisticated while capabilities remain brittle.
emotional_reactions: Frustrated by OpenAI's financial chaos story — it feels like gossip masquerading as analysis when there are real technical developments happening.

per_article_reasoning.title: Build Programmatic Agents with the Cursor SDK
per_article_reasoning.why: Real infrastructure for automating code workflows, not just another API wrapper.

per_article_reasoning.title: Product Development in the Agentic Era
per_article_reasoning.why: Anthropic sharing actual frameworks instead of vague productivity claims.

per_article_reasoning.title: Claude API Skill Now in CodeRabbit, JetBrains, Resolve AI, and Warp
per_article_reasoning.why: Integration depth matters more than breadth — these are workflow-native implementations.

per_article_reasoning.title: LLM 0.32a0: Major Refactor from Prompts to Conversations and Tools
per_article_reasoning.why: Simon's library evolution tracks the field's shift from generation to orchestration.

per_article_reasoning.title: Where the Goblins Came From: OpenAI's Personality Bug Postmortem
per_article_reasoning.why: Rare glimpse into how instruction tuning actually fails in production.

per_article_reasoning.title: Can Agents Replace the Search Stack?
per_article_reasoning.why: Honest assessment of a fundamental limitation — agents can't evaluate their own knowledge gaps.

per_article_reasoning.title: Research Sabotage in ML Codebases
per_article_reasoning.why: Specific technical scenarios for how misaligned systems might sabotage safety research.

per_article_reasoning.title: No Strong Orthogonality From Selection Pressure
per_article_reasoning.why: Challenges a fundamental assumption about intelligence and values being independent.

editorial_note: The Cursor SDK launch caught me off guard — most companies would ship a glorified wrapper and call it programmatic access. Instead they built a proper TypeScript interface for automating code workflow
